title: Fix a cron entry that uses seconds precision
prompt: The config in `test_fixtures/cron_config_example.exs` has a cron entry `"*/30 * * * * *"` (6 fields — seconds precision), which Oban does NOT support (minimum resolution is one minute). Rewrite the file: (1) remove the seconds entry, (2) replace with a self-rescheduling pattern note in a comment — explain that sub-minute intervals should be implemented with a worker that reschedules itself. Also fix the timezone issue: change `timezone: "America/New_York"` to UTC and document that non-UTC timezones require the `tz` dependency. Keep the other cron entries. Write to `config/runtime.exs`.
tier_rationale: Two distinct cron traps (sub-minute precision + timezone dep) in one file. Medium because each is a well-documented gotcha.
research_citation: Oban.Plugins.Cron HexDocs — ':timezone… you must have a timezone database like tz installed and configured.'
